scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF SALE AGREEMENT

1. Plaintiff Anita Sharma, aged 52 years, residing at Indiranagar, Bengaluru. 2. Defendant Rajesh Reddy, aged 60 years, residing at HSR Layout, Bengaluru. 3. Plaintiff and Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 in respect of Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for total consideration of Rs.85,00,000/-, under which the Plaintiff paid an advance of Rs.25,00,000/-. 4. The advance was paid by RTGS on the date of the agreement; RTGS details: UTR: HDFC20240110987654. 5. The Defendant undertook to execute the sale deed upon payment of the balance of Rs.60,00,000/-. 6. The Plaintiff was always ready and willing to perform her part of the contract.


JURISDICTION

2. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit; The Defendant is also a person sui juris and is liable to be sued. 2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try the present suit as the subject immovable property and the cause of action lie within Bengaluru; The Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit amount of Rs.85,00,000/- falls within the pecuniary limit of this Court; The Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits of this nature.


FACTS OF THE CASE

3. The Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 in respect of Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for total consideration of Rs.85,00,000/-, a true copy of which is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A. 4. In pursuance of the said agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance of Rs.25,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Lakh Only) via RTGS on the date of the agreement; RTGS details are evidenced by RTGS receipt marked as Annexure B. 5. As per the terms and conditions, the Defendant was obligated to execute the sale deed upon payment of the balance amount. However, on 05.06.2024 the Defendant sent a letter refusing to execute the sale deed, claiming that he had received a higher offer. 6. The Plaintiff served a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed; the legal notice is annexed as Annexure C along with postal receipt. 7. Despite the Plaintiff’s readiness and willingness to perform and despite service of legal notice, the Defendant refused to perform. The Plaintiff avers readiness to perform and has consistently acted in conformity with the contract; the documents constituting the contract and the communications evidencing readiness are Annexures A, B, C and D (Defendant’s refusal letter dated 05.06.2024).


LEGAL BASIS

4. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable to specific perform the contract under Section 20 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, inasmuch as the contract is supported by consideration, the property is immovable and within the jurisdiction of this Court, and the Plaintiff has performed her part (payment of advance and readiness to pay the balance) and the Defendant has refused to perform. 9. In the alternative, the Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable under Section 16 and Section 26(2) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, for granting relief in kind and for discretion in granting relief, given the circumstances of readiness and willingness and the breach by the Defendant. The Plaintiff also relies on the remedy under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 to the extent damages are claimed only as a fallback and not as the primary relief, noting that no damages are expressly claimed in the plaint except as may be incidentally considered if the court declines specific performance (with any damages to be subject to the proviso of Section 73).


CAUSE OF ACTION

5. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024, when the Defendant communicated refusal to execute the sale deed, and the same is a continuing cause of action which subsists till the date of realization of the sale deed. 11. The Plaintiff’s legal notice dated 20.06.2024 demonstrates readiness to perform and constitutes a part of the chain of acts demonstrating readiness and willingness.


LIMITATION

6. The suit is within the period of limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years from the date when the cause of action arose; in this case the accrual date is 05.06.2024, and the suit is filed within the period; the exact filing date to be inserted as {{FILING_DATE}}.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

7. The suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty-Five Lakh Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The requisite court fee is payable as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958; the precise amount of court fee shall be calculated and paid in accordance with the applicable statutory schedule (COURT FEE CONTEXT).


INTEREST

8. The Plaintiff claims interest as follows: (a) Pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the advance amount from the date of payment until the date of filing of this suit; (b) Pendente lite interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum during the pendency of this suit under Section 34 of the CPC; (c) Future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realization.


PRAYER

9. In the premises, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to: (a) decree specific performance in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant directing the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for the total consideration of Rs.85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakh Only) as per the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024; (b) direct the Defendant to receive the balance amount of Rs.60,00,000/- from the Plaintiff and to execute the sale deed and registration documents; (c) award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the advance amount from 10.01.2024 to the date of filing; (d) grant pendente lite interest at the rate deemed just and reasonable by this Court during the pendency of this suit; (e) grant future interest from the date of decree till realization; (f) award costs of this suit including the costs of issuing legal notices; (g) grant such other and further relief as this Court deems fit and proper.


LIST OF DOCUMENTS

10. Annexure A — Original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024. 2. Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654). 3. Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt. 4. Annexure D — Defendant’s refusal letter dated 05.06.2024.


VERIFICATION

I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 15 of this plaint are true to my personal knowledge and belief, that no part of the cause of action has arisen in any other court, and that I have not concealed any material facts. Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

Anita Sharma
Plaintiff

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____
TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, a company registered under the Companies Act, having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru, represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years, Occupation: Company, Residing at: MG Road, Bengaluru,

 ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP, a Limited Liability Partnership, having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru, represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years, Occupation: LLP, Residing at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,

 ... DEFENDANT

1. Plaintiff: TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, a company registered under the Companies Act, having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru, represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years, Occupation: Company.
2. Defendant: CloudBridge Systems LLP, a Limited Liability Partnership, having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru, represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years, Occupation: LLP.


JURISDICTION

3. This Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try the present suit as the contract between the parties was entered into in Bengaluru and the breach occurred within the local limits of Bengaluru. 2. The Plaintiff and Defendant conduct business within Bengaluru and the subject matter and cause of action relate to a contract executed and performed in Bengaluru; this Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain suits of this nature and the Commercial Court, Bengaluru, is the proper forum for reliefs sought herein.


FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a Software Development Agreement for development of an ERP system for a contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. 4. The Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as the first milestone via NEFT on 01.02.2024, the NEFT being evidenced by UTR ICIC20240201456789 (copy of NEFT receipt annexed as Annexure B). 5. As per the agreement, the Defendant was to deliver the first module by 01.05.2024. 6. The Defendant failed to deliver any module by the deadline and abandoned the project on 15.06.2024 without returning the advance paid by the Plaintiff. 7. The Plaintiff suffered an additional loss of Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations caused by non-delivery. 8. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to refund the advance and to compensate for the consequent losses; a copy of the legal notice is available with the record of the suit. 9. The Plaintiff submits a loss assessment report by an auditor establishing the consequential damages quantified at Rs. 12,00,000/- in addition to the refund of the advance of Rs. 20,00,000/-. 10. The Plaintiff claims total damages of Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rs. 20,00,000 refund of advance + Rs. 12,00,000 consequential damages) and interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the decretable amount until realization. 11. Evidence in support includes: Software Development Agreement (copy attached as Annexure A), NEFT receipt (Annexure B), email correspondence showing abandonment (Annexure C), and Loss Assessment Report (documentary evidence). 12. The Plaintiff prays for appropriate reliefs in this Commercial Court, Bengaluru.


LEGAL BASIS

5. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable for breach of contract by failing to deliver the contracted software module and abandoning the project, resulting in tangible damages and loss of business opportunities. The Plaintiff seeks damages in the amount of Rs. 32,00,000/-, which comprises Rs. 20,00,000/- already paid and to be refunded, and Rs. 12,00,000/- as consequential losses evidenced by the Loss Assessment Report. 9. The legal basis for damages for breach of contract is grounded in the general law of contract; the Plaintiff seeks monetary compensation for breach of contract arising from non-delivery and abandonment, which is recoverable in civil suits as damages. Where the breach occurs and there is no specific performance sought, the remedy remains damages for loss flowing from the breach. The Plaintiff also relies on the Limitation Act provisions for timely filing; Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 provides a three-year limitation for compensation for breach of contract not specially provided for. The accrual of the cause of action is the date of abandonment, i.e., 15.06.2024, and the suit is filed within the statutory period. 10. The Plaintiff reserves the right to amend the plaint if necessary to reflect any fresh or alternative legal basis as may be deemed appropriate by the Court.


CAUSE OF ACTION

6. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.06.2024, when the Defendant abandoned the project and failed to refund the advance, and it is a continuing cause of action until realization of the claim. 11. The legal notice dated 01.07.2024 was served pre-litigation but did not elicit any response, thereby justifying the institution of this suit for damages.


LIMITATION

7. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years from the date when the breach occurred. The accrual date is 15.06.2024 (the date of abandonment), and the present suit is filed within the prescribed period.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

8. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-two Lakh Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The court fee is payable ad valorem under the Karnataka Court Fees Act, with the exact quantum to be computed as per the relevant Schedule for damages claims in a Commercial Court proceeding. The Court Fees context for this value requires calculation under the applicable ad valorem rates by the Office of the Court.


INTEREST

9. Pre-suit interest: Plaintiff claims pre-suit interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the amount of Rs. 20,00,000/- from 01.02.2024 (the date of payment) or from the date of breach (15.06.2024) whichever is just, until the filing of this suit. 14. Pendente lite interest: Plaintiff prays for pendente lite interest at 18% per annum under Section 34 CPC (as deemed just by this Hon'ble Court) from the date of filing until decree. 15. Future interest: Plaintiff prays for interest at 18% per annum from the date of decree until actual realization.


PRAYER

10. In light of the above, this Hon'ble Court may graciously be pleased to:
    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-two Lakh Only) together with interest as claimed herein;
    (b) Award pre-suit interest on Rs. 32,00,000/- at the rate of 18% per annum from 15.06.2024 till filing;
    (c) Award pendente lite interest at 18% per annum during the pendency of this suit;
    (d) Award future interest from the date of decree till actual realization;
    (e) Award costs of this suit including pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing legal notice; and
    (f) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.


LIST OF DOCUMENTS

11. Software Development Agreement — Annexure A
12. NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789) — Annexure B
13. Email correspondence showing abandonment — Annexure C


VERIFICATION

I, Priya Menon, Director of TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 16 of this plaint are true to my personal knowledge and belief, that no part of the cause of action has arisen in any other court, and that I have not concealed any material facts. Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

PRIYA MENON
DIRECTOR, TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
